name: Enterprise Phishing to Domain Compromise
description: Attack tree modelling a phishing-based intrusion leading to full Active Directory domain compromise
root_objective: Achieve Domain Admin access through phishing-based initial access

nodes.title: Compromise Active Directory Domain
nodes.description: Achieve Domain Admin or equivalent privileged access to the enterprise Active Directory environment

nodes.title: Gain Initial Access via Phishing
nodes.description: Deliver malicious payload or harvest credentials through targeted phishing campaign

nodes.title: Spearphishing with Macro Document
nodes.description: Send targeted email with malicious Office document containing VBA macro payload

nodes.title: OAuth Consent Phishing
nodes.description: Trick user into granting OAuth permissions to attacker-controlled application for mailbox and data access

nodes.title: Escalate Privileges on Workstation
nodes.description: Elevate from standard user to local administrator on the compromised endpoint

nodes.title: Exploit Unpatched Local Privilege Escalation
nodes.description: Use known Windows local privilege escalation vulnerability to gain SYSTEM access

nodes.title: Harvest Domain Credentials
nodes.description: Obtain domain user and service account credentials from compromised systems

nodes.title: Dump LSASS Memory
nodes.description: Extract credential material from LSASS process memory using Mimikatz or comsvcs.dll

nodes.title: Kerberoasting
nodes.description: Request TGS tickets for SPNs and crack service account passwords offline

nodes.title: Achieve Domain Admin
nodes.description: Escalate to Domain Admin using harvested credentials or AD exploitation techniques

nodes.title: DCSync Attack
nodes.description: Use replication privileges to synchronize password hashes directly from Domain Controller

nodes.title: Golden Ticket / Pass-the-Hash
nodes.description: Forge Kerberos tickets or use NTLM hashes to authenticate as Domain Admin
